Sharing the updated slides with amendments to highlighted #s in below pages
- pg 11
- pg 19

Thanks and regards

Hi Yuna

My understanding on vn kyc hard block is system was launched, but need
buyer to update app in apple/google store to take effect (usually 4-14 days
to cover 80%-95% ado), which means, in coming days, not only historical
orders but all the orders before buyer update their app need to be
intercepted. Please consider whs space accordingly.

Shuning

Thanks.



Slide 4

   - When do we expect for VN orders to resume?
   - Why do we miss our P&L projections by so much?





Slide 5

   - #1 - What is the reason for the reduction of spend? If we account for
   this ADO/ADGMV loss, how will the YOY look like?
   - #2 – I do not quite understand what this is about



Slide 8

   - #2  - What is this about?



Slide 10

   - If I look at cost per acquisition and p&l/seller, is it worth it? Does
   it make sense to increase marketing spend to get more sellers?



Slide 11

   - How does this compare with our other channels of acquisition right
   now? For eg. BPO? 87k/month feels like a lot of money in general



Slide 13

   - How is TT and LZD managing this right now?
   - What is the method that we have adopted right now?
   - What is the difference between soft and hard block?



Slide 15

   - Why is the P&L for VN negative?



Slide 16 – 19

   - I need to understand how this will impact KRVN P&L for 2025 for the
   various options
   - I need to understand how this will impact entire KR P&L for 2025 for
   the various options

HI, Ian.
Following up on the item #5.

. Context : Last marketing growth analysis, we shared that overall MS spend
(paid-ad + CPAS + AMS) decreased in PH and VN and ROAS decreased in SG.
Today we're sharing only the deep dive into paid-ads and how we
transitioned to the different paid-ad type, which is GMV Max.

. Conclusion : Take rate is growing with stable YoY, and KR sellers moved
to GMV Max on par with platform level by this June. However, despite having
transitioned to GMV Max, SG MY PH have lower ROAS YoY and here are the
possible reasons.

(Hypothesis 1) KR sellers are investing more in auto bidding than in custom
ROAS within GMV Max, which is the opposite of the trend seen on the
platform and CNCB—where more investment should have gone into custom ROAS
mode.
(Hypothesis 2) KR sellers may have set their custom ROAS targets too
conservatively (i.e., too high).

Both hypotheses require further review of platform-wide and key
sellers-level data. so, our next step is to compare with platform's ROAS of
auto bidding vs custom mode to see if we should push for higher portion of
auto bidding spend only or also push for increasing the ROAS of custom
mode.
Further details can be found in the following three slides (also attached).
thanks!

[image: image.png]

[image: image.png][image: image.png]

Thanks.



If we compare KRCB vs CNCB Beauty sellers, is the take rate on par?



Should we push more KR sellers onto GMV Max, given that the ROAS is better?



If we look at the top 20 sellers, how has Ads Take Rate trend YOY? There
should not be reason to reduce ad spend if ROAS is improving in TH/TW/VN?

Hi, Ian.
Here are the feedback on your earlier questions.

If we compare KRCB vs CNCB Beauty sellers, is the take rate on par?

->

KRCB beauty 24 H1 : 4.5% -> 25 H1 : 5.7% (+1.2%p.)

CNCB beauty 24 H1 : 5.9% -> 25 H1 : 7.4% (+1.5%p.)

CNCB beauty's take-rate is higher than KRCB, which gives us room for
further push for paid-ad. However, we need to see the total marketing
investment take-rate including CPAS and AMS, so let us revisit it.


Should we push more KR sellers onto GMV Max, given that the ROAS is better?

->  Yes, especially onto GMV Max auto-bidding, which brings in better ROAS
in general.


If we look at the top 20 sellers, how has Ads Take Rate trend YOY? There
should not be reason to reduce ad spend if ROAS is improving in TH/TW/VN?

-> Their take-rate has been steadily increasing by 1.1% p. YoY, we have
more rooms to push in those 3 markets because their take-rate is still
lower vs SG/MY/PH which have lower ROAS.

thanks.

Hi Ian,

Based on last KR/JP bi-weekly meeting, VN team is looking into the
following the three areas:

1. YoY trend for KR vs. platform at category level

2. KR brand total business view

3. growth angles

Please review to our quick summary answer below (detail analysis kindly
refer to our material attached):

1. VN Platform ADO shows growing trend YoY in both ADO and ADG (+15% and
+26% respectively) while VN KRCB ADO is lagging behind in both metrics.

2. The significant decline is primarily attributed to (1) a decrease in the
makeup category, and (2) underperformance among several key brands.

   - Make-up category decline: Make-up -30% YoY, mainly impacted by 3CE
   drop, while other CATs remain stable.
   - Top brand's underperform: Stronger growth in brands' local retailers
   compared to CB shops among top 10 KR brands while lacking of new brands in
   pipeline

3. From VN market perspective, accelerating growth in the KR business will
be driven by strategic brand expansion, leveraging trend-driven
assortments, and enhancing localization efforts via 3PF and KR LS. These
key levers will enable us to capture market opportunities and strengthen
our competitive position.

Please let me know if you have further questions.

Regards,
Lisa

Hi, Ian.
earlier, i told you that SG/PH/MY had ROAS YoY decrease and further study
would be followed.
here are the feedbacks to that and also left comments in blue on your f/u
questions.
to summarize,* PH and MY* had ROAS issues in paid-ads in H1.

ㅁ SG: platform's paid-ad ROAS drop was even severer than KR (ROAS YoY SG
platform : -117% p. vs KRCB : -94% p.), so KR relatively well-defended.
ㅁ PH :
main cause - SMT 2 sellers increased paid-ad investment by an average of
151% YoY, leading to the massive ROAS drop.
*next steps* - for sellers with 50%+ YoY budget increase (4 sellers),
provide handpicked optimization consulting sessions (Recommend ad product
reallocation, daily budget adjustment, and revised ROAS target)
ㅁ MY :
main cause - 2 UST sellers (somebymi and VT cosmetics), they poorly managed
the manual mode and the shop ads
. Somebymi -  Manual ad ROAS dropped by -599%p despite a 22% budget
optimisation effort, shop ads saw a sharp cost surge.
. VT Cosmetics - Manual ad ROAS dropped due to a 113% increase in spend.
*next steps* - Propose early fadeout of manual ads and reallocate budget to
more efficient formats like GMV Max and content-based ads for further
testing and trial

If we compare KRCB vs CNCB Beauty sellers, is the take rate on par?

->

KRCB beauty 24 H1 : 4.5% -> 25 H1 : 5.7% (+1.2%p.)

CNCB beauty 24 H1 : 5.9% -> 25 H1 : 7.4% (+1.5%p.)

CNCB beauty's take-rate is higher than KRCB, which gives us room for
further push for paid-ad. However, we need to see the total marketing
investment take-rate including CPAS and AMS, so let us revisit it.

in general.

content.question: sellers with a high share of manual mode setting
sellers with GMV Max custom mode ROAS below 400%

content.answer:
seller segmentation & action mapping per market (completed by next week)
We'll finalize the segmentation into key action buckets:
[Group A] High Manual mode share
[Group B] GMV Max Custom ROAS < 400%
[Group C] YoY ad budget +50% with ROAS drop
Each seller will be tagged with recommended next steps (e.g. budget shift,
product change, format trial)

Execution tracking via shared dashboard (launching target : mid of Aug)
We will build a dashboard to track:
